level_signals: 7+ years experience in revenue operations or strategy
Partners with executive leadership and sales leadership
Highly visible role driving transformational programs

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years experience in revenue operations or strategy at a high-growth, technology company.
Have experience supporting planning processes such as long range planning, segmentation, budget planning, quota setting and sales compensation.
Structure complex problems, evaluate trade-offs, and synthesize recommendations that enable executive decision-making.
Build trust quickly and influence without direct authority across diverse stakeholder groups.
Communicate effectively with audiences ranging from frontline operators to executive leadership teams.
Are highly organized and capable of operating with high degrees of leverage while maintaining exceptional attention to detail.
Thrive in ambiguity and work autonomously.
Demonstrate sound judgment and an ownership mindset, proactively identifying risks and driving resolution.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Ideally bring experience with consumption-based, usage-based, or hybrid monetization models and understand the implications these models have on incentives, capacity planning, and go-to-market design.

scope.surface: GTM Strategy & Operations — revenue operating model, annual/quarterly planning cycles, segmentation, compensation design, and performance metrics
scope.business_model: enterprise AI sales with consumption/usage-based monetization elements
primary_metrics_inferred: GTM efficiency and growth at scale
core_responsibilities: Design and drive transformational programs that evolve revenue planning, compensation and operating model
Lead end-to-end execution of annual and quarterly go-to-market planning processes
Partner with Finance, Revenue Operations and business leaders to translate strategic objectives into executable plans
Develop and operationalize program frameworks across market segmentation, coverage, capacity, target setting, sales compensation, and performance metrics
Structure complex problems, evaluate trade-offs, and synthesize recommendations for executive decision-making
Build integrated planning timelines, governance mechanisms, and cross-functional operating rhythms
Identify process inefficiencies and opportunities for automation, simplification, and standardization
Partner with Enablement and change leaders on adoption
Monitor program outcomes and incorporate lessons learned

green_flags: Highly visible role with direct partnership with Sales Leadership and executive stakeholders
Clear articulation of scope and responsibilities
Encourages candidate self-selection with 'you might thrive if' framing rather than rigid requirements list
OpenAI's scale and mission provide significant program impact opportunity
red_flags: No location or remote/hybrid policy stated — work arrangement is entirely unclear
Role title says 'Program Manager' but scope covers strategy, operations, and compensation design — potential title/scope mismatch
7+ year floor with transformational program ownership suggests high expectations not fully reflected in title

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required, program scope, and executive visibility)
scope.business_model (inferred from references to consumption-based models and GTM context)
location.type set to 'unclear' — no location or work model mentioned in JD
location.work_auth set to 'open' — no restriction stated
ambiguities: The JD references San Francisco and Los Angeles Fair Chance Ordinances in boilerplate, suggesting US-based roles in CA, but no explicit city or location requirement is stated for this role specifically.

The 'You might thrive in this role if' section lists items that function as requirements (e.g. 7+ years experience) but uses softer framing — treated as hard must-haves given the specificity and placement as primary qualifications.
